title: Chargers troll Patriots over Mike Vrabel, Dianna Russini, and more in schedule release video
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Chargers' schedule-release video featured jokes targeting Patriots coach Mike Vrabel and former reporter Dianna Russini.
Los Angeles' Halo-themed video included references to the Patriots' 2025 Super Bowl loss and a lighter regular-season schedule.
The Chargers play the Patriots on November 29 during Sunday Night Football at SoFi Stadium.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a quiz to test knowledge on recent Greater Boston news stories.
The quiz is designed to help residents stay informed about trending local events.
General Assignment Editor Peter Chianca has been with Boston.com since 2019.

title: Review and setlist: Courtney Barnett shows that being a ‘creature of habit’ isn’t always a bad thing
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Courtney Barnett performed at Roadrunner on May 13, promoting her fourth album 'Creature of Habit' with a 70-minute set.
Barnett balanced new tracks and hits like 'Avant Gardener' with minimal stage banter, focusing on her music and guitar skills.
Opening acts included Brooklyn's Momma and Truman Sinclair, with Momma performing songs from their album 'Welcome to My Blue Sky.'

title: MassGOP-backed lieutenant governor candidate fails to collect enough signatures to make ballot
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Anne Brensley, GOP-endorsed candidate for lieutenant governor, failed to collect the required 10,000 signatures for the ballot.
Brensley's campaign alleges forgery concerns and rejected batches of signatures after paying Joe Bronske for gathering efforts.
The Massachusetts Secretary of State's office received forgery concerns; deadline extension requests for signature collection were denied.

title: 7 takeaways from Patriots’ 2026 schedule
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots' 2026 schedule includes six nationally televised games and a game in Germany against the Detroit Lions.
They open the season with a Super Bowl LX rematch against the Seahawks on September 9th in Seattle.
New England will face eight 2025 playoff teams and has the sixth-hardest schedule based on opponents' 2025 win percentages.

title: Here’s your chance to design a new Boston Public Library card
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston Public Library launches a card design contest from May 15 to June 15.
Submissions are open to three age groups: youth, teens, and adults, with winners to be featured on library cards.
Designs should reflect the library's community impact, with originality required; AI-generated works are not allowed.

title: ‘This was my baby’: Blaze destroys popular Belmont ice cream shop, causes floor to collapse
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A three-alarm fire destroyed Moozy's Ice Cream Emporium in Belmont early Thursday morning, causing the floor to collapse.
Firefighters from neighboring towns assisted, and one firefighter sustained a minor injury but continued working.
The cause of the fire is under investigation; neighboring businesses suffered smoke and water damage.

title: Patriots officially unveil 2026 schedule that begins with Super Bowl rematch
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots' 2026 season opens with a Super Bowl rematch against the Seahawks in Seattle on September 9.
New England's 2026 schedule features six nationally televised games, including a match in Munich, Germany with the Lions.
The Patriots will host their home opener against the Steelers at Gillette Stadium on September 20.

title: Did my vehicle miss out on an important safety recall?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2009 VW Jetta SportWagen owner missed a 2017 recall notice for an ABS module update due to VIN exclusions.
Owners missing recall notices can seek reimbursement from manufacturers like Volkswagen by contacting their customer service or NHTSA.
Recalls often target specific production runs; affected owners should verify eligibility and pursue potential reimbursements with documentation.

title: Bruins assistant GM leaving organization to ‘pursue other opportunities’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins assistant GM Jamie Langenbrunner leaves after 11 years to explore opportunities elsewhere in the NHL.
Langenbrunner joined the Bruins in 2015 and became assistant GM in 2022, focusing on player personnel and acquisitions.
Potential replacements include Adam McQuaid and Zdeno Chara for Langenbrunner's role within the Bruins' front office.
